summary: Large item-system matching pass that raised all item translation units above the PR's >90% target, with 28 files touched and several item object files promoted from NonMatching to Matching. The automated decomp.dev report recorded +12020 matched code bytes, +20644 linked code bytes, 43 new matches, and 30 additional unmatched-item improvements, concentrated in it_2725, itcoll, and item-specific TUs such as Samus charge shot, Game & Watch chef, Kamex, Unknown, Houou, and Whitebea. The diff also improved item/fighter shared type knowledge, including replacing HSD_ObjAllocUnk2 with a named Item_FtTrack and adding/adjusting item attribute and item-var structures.

key_files.why: Promoted multiple item source files from NonMatching to Matching, including itsamuschargeshot.c, itgamewatchchef.c, itkamex.c, itunknown.c, ithouou.c, and itwhitebea.c.
Largest code-matching rewrite in the PR; decomp.dev reported many new matches and near-matches here, including it_80279E24, it_80279544, it_80279FF8, it_80278800, and it_802790C0.
Substantial item collision cleanup and matching improvements; reported new matches include it_8026FE68 and it_802701BC, with several collision routines improved above 90%.
Added/refined shared item data types, including Item_FtTrack, ItHurtBoneDesc, ItHurtBoneList, and DamageLogEntry comments/renaming.
Refined item attribute and item-var layouts for Kamex, Houou, Whitebea, Oldottosea, and Unknown, including unions where fields are used as both integer and float data.
Refined Link boomerang variables and attributes, adding typed animation/joint fields and replacing generic x0/x4/x8 names with timer/start/end semantics.
Changed ftCo_80098634 to take Item_FtTrack* and write x150_count, tying fighter down-attack tracking to the newly named item/fighter tracking structure.
Adjusted Link boomerang call signature to pass an additional angle argument to it_802A0534.
Updated symbol map for newly matched data, including converting many formerly global it_804D*/it_803F* constants to local @ labels and correcting one data object size/label layout.

what_changed: Changed build configuration so six item TUs moved from NonMatching to Matching: itsamuschargeshot.c, itgamewatchchef.c, itkamex.c, itunknown.c, ithouou.c, and itwhitebea.c.

Performed broad matching rewrites in src/melee/it/it_2725.c and src/melee/it/itcoll.c, reducing deleted placeholder/nonmatching code and replacing it with C that decomp.dev recognized as new matches or major improvements.
Matched item-specific behavior/data in itlinkboomerang.c, itsamuschargeshot.c, itwhitebea.c, itunknown.c, itsscope.c, ithouou.c, itkamex.c, and itgamewatchchef.c.
Replaced the generic forward declaration HSD_ObjAllocUnk2 with Item_FtTrack and propagated that name through ftCo_80098634, Item_804A0CCC declarations, and item collision users.
Renamed Item_FtTrack field x150 to x150_count and documented it as the count of populated per-fighter ECB/position entries.

Added comments and explicit structs for item hurtbone descriptors/lists, noting that ItHurtBoneDesc mirrors HurtCapsule offset/scale fields with a leading bone_id and is copied into Item::xACC_itemHurtbox by it_8027163C.

Refined item attribute structures with better field interpretations, including itHouou_ItemVars, itKamexAttributes timer/min/max-style fields, itLinkBoomerangAttributes typed joint/animation fields, and itUnknown fields represented as unions when the same slot is used as f32 or s32.
Adjusted GALE01 symbol labels so many constants/data objects became local @ labels rather than global it_804D*/it_803F* symbols, improving data matching and symbol-map accuracy.

Used matching aids visible in the diff, including PAD_STACK adjustments, #pragma dont_inline regions, static inline helper extraction, and deliberate static constants such as U32_TO_F32/S32_TO_F32-related f64 values.
smart_moves: Named a previously generic allocator-like blob as Item_FtTrack only after evidence connected it to ftCo_80098634 population and it_80271B60 item/fighter ECB overlap reads; this turns an unknown HSD_ObjAllocUnk2 into a reusable domain type.

Preserved ambiguous multi-use data slots with unions rather than forcing a single incorrect type, especially in itUnknown item vars/attributes where fields are accessed as both float and integer values.
Updated symbols.txt alongside C/data matching so constants that compile as local labels are not left as misleading global symbols.
Promoted files to Matching in configure.py only for TUs that the diff and automated report show as fully matched, keeping remaining low-confidence units in NonMatching.
Used explicit count naming, x150_count, for array traversal bounds in Item_FtTrack, improving readability without changing layout.
Documented structural relationships in types.h, such as the relationship between ItHurtBoneDesc and HurtCapsule, which helps later decompilers avoid re-deriving the same layout.
decomp_lessons: When an unknown global struct is populated in fighter code and consumed by item collision code, rename and place it in shared item headers only after both producer and consumer evidence agree on the layout.
For data matching, symbol scope matters: constants emitted as local @ labels should be represented that way in symbols.txt instead of keeping address-derived global names.
Item attribute tables often need precise pointer/animation/joint field types to unlock data matches; changing only code may not be enough.
Union fields are appropriate evidence-grounded placeholders when the same item attribute or item-var slot participates in both integer and floating-point operations.
Small stack and inlining differences still matter for late-stage matching; PAD_STACK changes and #pragma dont_inline boundaries were part of the matching strategy in this PR.
Replacing unnecessary HSD_GObj casts with typed Item_GObj accessors can improve both readability and matching when typedefs/macros already carry the correct type.
Large matching passes should update build configuration, headers, source, and symbol maps together; this PR's gains came from treating code, data, and type information as coupled.
naming_conventions: Use domain-specific names for formerly generic HSD_ObjAllocUnk* structs when evidence exists; this PR replaced HSD_ObjAllocUnk2 with Item_FtTrack.
Name count fields with a _count suffix when they bound array iteration, as in x150_count.
Use item-specific struct names in the pattern it<Name>Attributes and it<Name>_ItemVars, e.g. itHouou_ItemVars, itKamexAttributes, itWhiteBeaAttributes, and itUnknown_ItemVars.
For still-unknown fields, preserve offset-based names such as x64, x68, x78, but use unions or comments to encode observed use instead of inventing semantic names.
Use local @NNN labels in symbols.txt for local compiler-emitted constants/strings/floats/doubles rather than address-based global it_804D*/it_803F* names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Adjusted PAD_STACK sizes in several functions to recover stack layout-sensitive matches.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around selected functions in it_2725.c to control inlining behavior.
Introduced static inline helper extraction, such as an it_80279E24_inline helper, to match repeated codegen patterns while preserving call-site structure.
Materialized static floating constants for conversion helpers, including U32_TO_F32 and S32_TO_F32-related f64 symbols, where data matching required them.
Changed command parsing to use typed CommandInfo/union-style access patterns and explicit u16/u8 bit extraction in item command handlers.
Converted unnecessary casts like GET_ITEM((HSD_GObj*) item_gobj) to GET_ITEM(item_gobj), relying on the typed Item_GObj alias and affecting generated code/readability.
Updated data symbol scope and sizes to align emitted .data/.sdata/.sdata2 with the matched objects.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

Automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 showed a net positive result: matched code 64.94% (+0.31%, +12020 bytes), linked code 37.07% (+0.53%, +20644 bytes), matched data 38.55% (+0.10%, +1232 bytes), and linked data 32.85% (+0.08%, +1008 bytes).
The automated report listed 43 new matches and 30 improvements in unmatched items, with one small unmatched-item data regression in main/melee/it/it_2725 .data from 93.58% to 93.56%.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish matching it_2725 .data after the small regression reported here.
Search for Item_FtTrack usages to see whether later work further named x0_ecb_arr, xC0_pos_arr, or x154 flags.
Search for itUnknown_ItemVars and itUnknownAttributes to see if union fields x64/x68/x78/x0/x18/x1C/x20 gained semantic names.
Review later itlinkboomerang.c changes around it_802A0534 and angle passing from ftLk_SpecialS.
Find PRs that continued converting global it_804D*/it_803F* constants to local @ labels for item data matching.
